_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for the human ear (Class 11 Neural Control and Coordination -- sense organs). Image keyed via prepare_illustration.py's new 'white' background mode (added 2026-07-15) -- the source PNG had an opaque white background despite the prompt asking for solid deep-charcoal #121316 (visible live in ChatGPT's UI as dark, but the downloaded raster was flattened onto white). Hotspots authored by visual inspection of the cropped/keyed webp, NOT yet browser-verified live -- recommend a quick pass in the prototype to confirm tap-target placement before shipping, especially any nested/adjacent structures.
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Pinna (outer ear)
hotspots.detail: The visible, cartilage-supported outer part that funnels sound waves into the ear canal.

hotspots.label: Ear canal
hotspots.detail: Carries sound waves from the pinna to the eardrum.

hotspots.label: Tympanic membrane (eardrum)
hotspots.detail: A thin membrane that vibrates in response to sound waves.

hotspots.label: Ossicles
hotspots.detail: Three tiny bones — malleus, incus, stapes — that amplify and relay vibrations to the inner ear.

hotspots.label: Semicircular canals
hotspots.detail: Fluid-filled loops that detect rotational movement of the head, helping maintain balance.

hotspots.label: Cochlea
hotspots.detail: The spiral, fluid-filled structure that converts sound vibrations into nerve impulses.

hotspots.label: Eustachian tube
hotspots.detail: Connects the middle ear to the throat, equalising air pressure on both sides of the eardrum.

hotspots.label: Auditory nerve
hotspots.detail: Carries signals from the cochlea to the brain for interpretation as sound.

generation_prompt: A clean modern educational anatomy illustration of the human ear, shown as a coronal cross-section revealing its full internal structure from outer to inner ear. Show these structures clearly separated and individually distinguishable: pinna (outer ear), ear canal, tympanic membrane (eardrum), the three ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes), cochlea, semicircular canals, Eustachian tube, and auditory nerve. Style: semi-realistic medical-textbook illustration, soft even studio lighting, smooth clean surfaces, a muted anatomical palette. Calm and flat: no glossy highlights, no neon, no glow, no 3D-render sheen. Composition: a single ear in side cross-section, centered and filling the frame, generous even margins. Solid uniform deep-charcoal background, hex #121316, edge to edge. Absolutely no text, no labels, no numbers, no letters, no leader lines, no arrows, no legend, no watermark -- the illustration only. Landscape 4:3, high resolution.
